5 
I INTRODUCTION 
 
I.1 Product name  
blksea_omi_tempsal_sst_area_averaged_anomalies 
This OMI product includes unfiltered SST anomaly, given as monthly mean time 
series starting on 1982 and averaged over the Black Sea, and filtered SST 
anomaly (Tt), obtained by using the X11 -seasonal adjustmen t procedure.  
Anomalies are computed against the 1991-2020 reference period. 
 
blksea_omi_tempsal_sst_trend 
This OMI product includes the cumulative/net trend map for the Black Sea, i.e. 
the rate of change of SST (°C/year) scaled by the number of time steps 
(namely, the number of years). Trend computation is based on X -11 seasonal 
adjustment procedure. 
I.2 Indicator evaluation method 
The Black Sea (BLKSEA) Sea Surface Temperature (SST) Ocean Monitoring 
Indicators (OMIs) are derived from the CMEMS Reprocessed Black Sea L4 (BS-
REP-L4) SST product and its update runs in parallel with the corresponding BS-
REP-L4 update. The BS-REP-L4 product provides the SSTs that are used to 
compute all the Black Sea SST OMIs, i.e. the time series of SST anomalies 
(unfiltered and filtered, blksea_omi_sst_area_averaged_anomalies) and the 2D 
map of SST trend (blksea_omi_sst_trend). Anomalies are computed aga inst the 
1991-2020 reference period. The 30 -year climatology 1991 -2020 is defined 
according to the WMO recommendation (WMO, 2017) and recent U.S. 
National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration practice
